records.title: Tateishi Nakamise / Keisei Tateishi north-side retro shopping streets
records.summary: Retro shopping streets around Keisei Tateishi are under strong redevelopment pressure as the north-side district redevelopment project advances and long-running family businesses face rising pressure to exit.
records.sourceLabel: Katsushika Ward redevelopment overview + reporting on Tateishi Nakamise pressure

records.title: Nishiogikubo south-side small-business streets / Willow Alley
records.summary: The intimate small-business streets south of Nishiogikubo Station, including Willow Alley, face an uncertain future amid rising rents, road expansion and redevelopment pressure.
records.sourceLabel: Japan Times on redevelopment and resistance in Nishiogikubo

records.title: GiGO Akihabara Building 1
records.summary: A landmark Akihabara arcade with over 30 years of history that officially closed on August 31, 2025, making it a core 'recently lost' reference record.
records.sourceLabel: GENDA GiGO official closure notice

records.title: Cinema Qualite
records.summary: A long-running Shinjuku mini-theater that entered the 2025 wave of Tokyo independent cinema closures, suitable as a high-signal 'recently lost' film-culture record.
records.sourceLabel: Japan Times / Time Out Tokyo closure signals for Cinema Qualite

records.title: Imperial Theatre
records.summary: The historic Imperial Theatre has been closed from February 28, 2025 until further notice, making it a major Tokyo cultural-space loss/rebuild reference record.
records.sourceLabel: GO TOKYO closure notice for Imperial Theatre

records.title: Osaka Shochikuza Theatre
records.summary: The century-old Osaka Shochikuza Theatre in Dotonbori is scheduled to close indefinitely in 2026 after its May production run due to the ageing condition of its facilities.
records.sourceLabel: Time Out Osaka report on indefinite closure

records.title: Airin Labor and Welfare Center
records.summary: The Airin Labor and Welfare Center, long central to Kamagasaki’s day-laborer district, was closed earlier and demolished after the 2024 eviction, making it a core recently-lost social-history record for Osaka.
records.sourceLabel: Le Monde report on Kamagasaki erasure and Airin Center demolition

records.title: Namba Sennichimae redevelopment area
records.summary: The Namba Sennichimae district is entering a large mixed-use redevelopment cycle, putting the pre-redevelopment streetscape and small-business texture of the area under pressure.
records.sourceLabel: Estie summary of Namba Sennichimae Redevelopment Project

records.title: Expo 2025 Osaka site temporary pavilion landscape
records.summary: The temporary pavilion landscape of Expo 2025 Osaka began entering dismantling and reuse workflows after the event closed, making the intact expo-site condition a time-limited record.
records.sourceLabel: Expo official reuse / dismantling notices

records.title: South Gate Building pre-renovation commercial floors
records.summary: The South Gate Building directly connected to Osaka Station is entering its first major full-floor renovation cycle, making the pre-renewal commercial interior a transitional Osaka record.
records.sourceLabel: Konrad summary of South Gate Building major renovation plan

records.title: Curzon Mayfair
records.summary: One of London's oldest West End cinemas is closing as redevelopment proceeds, making the current Curzon Mayfair an immediate last-chance film venue record.
records.sourceLabel: Time Out London report on Curzon Mayfair closure and redevelopment

records.title: Prince Charles Cinema
records.summary: The Prince Charles Cinema has faced a serious closure threat linked to lease pressure and redevelopment risk, making it a high-signal live candidate for Last Chances.
records.sourceLabel: The Guardian report on Prince Charles Cinema closure threat

records.title: Dar al-Taqwa Bookshop
records.summary: London's oldest independent Islamic bookshop is reportedly at risk of closure within a year, making it a strong live candidate for cultural-space preservation mapping.
records.sourceLabel: The Guardian report on Dar al-Taqwa closure risk

records.title: Corsica Studios
records.summary: Corsica Studios announced a 2026 closure tied to redevelopment of the site after more than two decades as a core London underground music venue.
records.sourceLabel: The Standard report on Corsica Studios 2026 closure

records.title: Smithfield Market
records.summary: Smithfield Market is set to continue operating only until 2028 before closure and redevelopment plans reshape the historic wholesale market site.
records.sourceLabel: The Guardian report on Smithfield redevelopment and planned closure timeline

records.title: Bluestockings Cooperative
records.summary: The long-running feminist and radical community bookstore Bluestockings announced that it would close after 26 years, making it a clear recently-lost NYC cultural-space record.
records.sourceLabel: Time Out New York report on Bluestockings closure

records.title: Music Hall of Williamsburg
records.summary: Music Hall of Williamsburg is expected to lose its lease at the end of 2026, putting one of Brooklyn’s defining mid-sized venues on a last-chance clock.
records.sourceLabel: Time Out New York report on Music Hall of Williamsburg lease loss

records.title: Studio 54
records.summary: Studio 54 may be forced to close within the next couple of years unless zoning relief unlocks renovation funding, making it a strong live-risk theater record.
records.sourceLabel: Time Out New York report on Studio 54 closure threat

records.title: Tin Building
records.summary: The Tin Building at South Street Seaport officially shut down in early 2026, making it a clear recently-lost food-market and waterfront commercial-space record.
records.sourceLabel: Time Out New York report on Tin Building shutdown

records.title: The Pleasure Chest West Village
records.summary: The Pleasure Chest’s West Village location closed in February 2026, ending a long neighborhood chapter for one of New York’s best-known sex-positive retail spaces.
records.sourceLabel: Time Out New York report on The Pleasure Chest West Village closure
